meta_title: For Freelancers: analytics setup to Solve stuck with basic apps (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Analytics Setup to Solve App Stagnation
content.seo_description: Stop building apps that go nowhere. Learn a professional analytics setup to track user behavior and scale your freelance projects using Antigravity and Claude.
content.answer_block: To move beyond basic apps, you must implement event-driven analytics. By integrating a lightweight tracking layer, you gain the data needed to justify your value to clients. Use Antigravity to scaffold your tracking infrastructure and Claude Code to automate the deployment of event hooks across your entire codebase immediately.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks coding, polishing the UI, and shipping on time, but the silence from your client’s dashboard is deafening. You are stuck in the 'basic app' trap—building features that look good but lack the telemetry to prove user engagement. Without data, you are just a developer guessing at requirements.

It is time to pivot from being a code-monkey to a growth-partner. By integrating a professional-grade analytics stack, you transform your deliverables into data-driven assets. This guide shows you how to leverage Antigravity and Claude Code to automate the instrumentation of your apps, turning every click into actionable intelligence.

steps.title: Define Your Telemetry Schema
steps.description: Before writing code, map out the critical user flows. You need to track 'conversion' events, not just page views. We define the schema to ensure consistent data ingestion.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a telemetry schema definition file for a SaaS app. Include events for user_signup, feature_click, and session_duration. Use a standard JSON structure.
steps.claude_code_command: claude create --file schema.json --context 'Generate a typescript interface based on this schema'

steps.title: Scaffold the Analytics Provider
steps.description: Inject a provider wrapper that decouples your application logic from the analytics vendor. This ensures you can switch providers without rewriting your entire codebase.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust AnalyticsProvider class in TypeScript that handles event buffering and batching to minimize network overhead.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit --file src/lib/analytics.ts --prompt 'Implement a singleton pattern with batching logic'

steps.title: Automate Event Hook Injection
steps.description: Manually adding tracking to every button is tedious. Use Claude Code to scan your components and inject tracking hooks automatically.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a regex-based script to find all onClick handlers in my components and wrap them with a trackEvent call.

steps.title: Configure Server-Side Proxying
steps.description: Avoid ad-blockers by proxying analytics events through your own backend. This ensures 100% data capture rate for your clients.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Next.js API route that acts as a secure proxy to forward client-side events to the analytics endpoint.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add --file src/app/api/track/route.ts --logic 'validate origin and forward headers'

steps.title: Validate Data Pipeline
steps.description: Before shipping, verify that your events are firing correctly and reaching the destination in real-time.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a test suite to simulate user clicks and verify that the analytics provider receives the correct payload.

pro_tips: Always use a unique session ID stored in local storage to track user journeys across multiple days.
Never track PII (Personally Identifiable Information) directly; map them to internal UUIDs before sending.
Implement a 'debug mode' flag that logs analytics events to the browser console for instant development feedback.
Use a 'dead-man's switch' in your analytics provider to disable tracking if the latency exceeds 50ms.
Create a custom dashboard view for your clients that highlights only the top 3 KPIs, ignoring vanity metrics.
content.next_steps: Deploy your telemetry stack to a staging environment and run a smoke test to ensure your event payloads are clean. Once verified, integrate this pattern into your standard freelance boilerplate to save 5+ hours on every future project.
content.conversion_block: Stop building in the dark and start shipping apps that provide measurable ROI. Elevate your freelance career by mastering the tools that top-tier engineers use daily. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
